title: Tangy Juicy German Pork Stew
description: A hearty, diabetic-friendly German stew featuring tender pork, tangy flavors, and a hint of sesame seed oil, perfect for a lazy Sunday. This dish is carefully crafted to be low in carbs and rich in protein and healthy fats.
instructions: Heat the sesame seed oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the pork cubes and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove the pork from the pot and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until it starts to caramelize, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the apple cider vinegar, chicken broth, caraway seeds, salt, and black pepper to the pot. Stir to combine.
Return the pork to the pot and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 hours, or until the pork is tender.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.
